project.title: Phosphorylation of GABAAR α2 subunit
project.projectDescription: γ-aminobutyric acid type A receptors (GABAARs) mediate fast synaptic inhibition. Phosphorylation governs GABAARs function and trafficking in α4, β and γ subunits. However, phosphorylation of other subunits has not been demonstrated. Here we show that the α2 subunit is phosphorylated on S359.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: To identify phosphorylation sites on GABAAR α2 subunit, we used a previously studied myc/pHluorin tagged GABAAR α2 subunit (pHα2) knock-in mice (10.1074/jbc.M116.724443). pHα2 was immunoprecipitated from brain lysates with GFP-Trap, run on a SDS-PAGE gel, and the ~80kDa band corresponding to pHα2 was exised and sent to Taplin MS Facility (Harvard) for trypsin/chymotrypsin digestion and analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Analysis was performed using ReAdW.exe (ver4.3.1), SEQUEST (ver. 28, rev 13). Peptides were required to be tryptic. Fixed modifications considered were 57.0215 Da on cysteine. Variable modifications considered: methionine oxidation and phosphorylation on serine, threonine and tyrosine. Mass tolerance was set to 2 Da for precursor ions and 1 Da for fragment ions. Ascore was used to determine the confidence of a given phosphorylation site.

references.referenceLine: Nakamura Y, Morrow DH, Nathanson AJ, Henley JM, Wilkinson KA, Moss SJ. Phosphorylation on Ser 359 of the α2 subunit in GABA type A receptors down-regulates their density at inhibitory synapses. J Biol Chem. 2020
